data.body: TRUNCATION follow-ups (Joe's 4 points). #1 NO CAP BY DESIGN (a cap invalidates the review) — upgraded ca03 from 'by default'. #2 WHOLE-SET criteria (G3/G4) need parent+ALL children together; can't review child-in-isolation -> over-budget = MAP-REDUCE over children (full bodies) + deterministic aggregation (G3 union of covered ACs; G4 within/cross-batch interaction modes), never truncate a child; INDETERMINATE only if uncoverable. #3 MISSING-INFO FPs: default chunks the RUBRIC not the CONTENT, so finders keep the WHOLE plan (no partial-view FP); when content MUST be decomposed, absence/'missing X' findings are CANDIDATES verified by Pass-2 against the COMPLETE artifact (higher bar) — the finder may see a slice but the VERIFIER must have whole-artifact access (added the rule to PASS2_SYSTEM). #5 CONTEXT-WINDOW ESCALATION: we did NOT have one — added gate_lib.pick_model (smallest model whose window fits) + with_context_escalation (catch context-limit error -> escalate up the ladder) BEFORE decomposing; largest-window-exceeded -> decompose/INDETERMINATE, never cap. All captured in ca03 + harness. Committed.
